one_time_description: Large bad debt expense of $1,481,757 recorded to correct errors in accounts receivable sub-ledger system
economic_notes: Nation has significant business interests through FM Business Trust including Christina River Enterprises Limited Partnership, FMFN Rigs Limited Partnership, CRE/NCSG Crane and Heavy Haul Limited Partnership, and others. FM Business Trust reported total revenue of $30,767,937 and total expenses of $33,088,338 for the year. Nation also received Agricultural Benefit Trust distributions and industry relations revenues. COVID-19 noted as having significant operational impact.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021, not 2024. Auditor firm name not explicitly stated — report signed 'Chartered Professional Accountants' from Edmonton, Alberta with no firm name provided. Nation consolidates 5 entities including Industrial Relations Corporation, Community Trust, Office of Commercial Development Ltd., Business Association Ltd., and Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust. Five additional business entities accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs). Significant loss of $2,701,739 from investments in Nation partnerships and businesses (primarily FM Business Trust). Large bad debt expense of $1,481,757 recorded to correct sub-ledger errors. ISC Native Claims Loans of $1,113,957 are interest-free contingent loans not included in long-term debt total. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $12,081,024 + ISC Health $2,665,674 + CMHC $24,331 + Other government funding $199,998 + ATC $240,028 + FNDF $248,122 = $15,459,177 (approximate, ATC and FNDF may not be government transfers). Related party transaction with company owned by former Councillor Ronald Kreutzer Jr. for $265,038.
